fault_hypothesis: On line 69, the condition `now > adjusted_expiry` uses strict greater-than, so when `now == adjusted_expiry` (i.e., the token is exactly at expiry), the token is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired. The condition should be `now >= adjusted_expiry`.

constraints.intent: When now equals adjusted_expiry, the strict > comparison fails to detect expiry, so the condition should be >= but is >; this expr is FALSE when now==adjusted_expiry (failing tests) and TRUE otherwise (passing tests).
Sanity check that adjusted_expiry__1 is defined and comparable to now; always true but ensures the variable is in scope for further checks.

Before using adjusted_expiry__1 in the comparison, now must be strictly less than adjusted_expiry__1 for the token to be valid; when now==adjusted_expiry__1 this is FALSE, matching the failing tests where the token should be expired.
In the else branch (token returned as valid), now must be strictly less than adjusted_expiry__1; when now==adjusted_expiry__1 this is FALSE, indicating the boundary case is mishandled.
